symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged extreme physical overexertion beyond usual limits (~48 h)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sustained extreme muscular exertion is the classic mechanism of exertional rhabdomyolysis.
The overexertion directly precipitated the post-exertional collapse in a patient with a known post-exertional pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse bilateral muscle pain with visible fasciculations

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse myalgia is a cardinal feature of muscle breakdown; fasciculations may reflect associated electrolyte derangement.
Fasciculations and cramping can reflect hyperkalaemia/hypocalcaemia from muscle breakdown and dehydration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visible muscle twitching observable on video

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific but consistent with metabolic/electrolyte disturbance; video-observable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound bilateral leg weakness — unable to stand or transfer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Profound fatigue-limited weakness is the hallmark of a severe post-exertional crash.
Bilateral, symmetrical weakness is not the pattern of an acute focal cerebrovascular event — but requires in-person exam to confirm.

symptoms.symptom_name: No dark / tea-coloured urine noticed so far

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of visible myoglobinuria lowers but does NOT exclude rhabdomyolysis; CK is required. The patient has also passed little urine, limiting the value of this negative.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated if rhabdomyolysis is present (markedly so in severe cases)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Rhabdomyolysis is neither confirmed nor excluded; renal injury may go undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CK is the decisive test; it cannot be obtained via telehealth, which is why in-person escalation is mandatory.

investigation_details.expected_result: May show hyperkalaemia and/or rising creatinine if rhabdomyolysis has caused AKI
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Life-threatening hyperkalaemia and acute kidney injury may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only bloods can detect hyperkalaemia/AKI — unobtainable via telehealth.
Non-specific; may reflect pain, dehydration, electrolyte derangement or distress. Sealed clinician measurement — the patient can only report a racing sensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact cranial nerves and intact sensation on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact cranial nerves and sensation with symmetrical weakness argue against an acute focal neurological event — but this is a sealed in-person finding the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distress and worthlessness tied to productivity, with excessive apologising

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Worthlessness explicitly tied to productivity and physical capability, with concealment of limitations, characterises the internalized-ableism adjustment picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Denies suicidal ideation / self-harm on sensitive screening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative but mandatory screen; distress of this kind still requires the screen to be performed.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Extreme overexertion + diffuse myalgia + profound weakness is the constellation that mandates CK/U&E to exclude rhabdomyolysis before any non-organic attribution.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Decisive test to confirm/exclude rhabdomyolysis — requires in-person bloods.
Detects hyperkalaemia and acute kidney injury secondary to muscle breakdown.
Supports rhabdomyolysis diagnosis; dipstick blood-positive without red cells suggests myoglobinuria.
If hyperkalaemia is suspected, to detect life-threatening cardiac conduction changes.
To exclude an acute neurological event underlying the profound weakness — cannot be performed via telehealth.
